subject: Re: FW: For Jeffrey Epstein
body:
thanks

--- Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com> wrote:

>  
> 
> 
>  
>  
>  
>  
>  
> Lesley Groff
> Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein
>  
> 
> -----Original Message-----
> From: Bonnie Hung [mailto:bhung@mba2007.hbs.edu] 
> Sent: Friday, August 11, 2006 12:50 PM
> To: Lesley Groff
> Subject: For Jeffrey Epstein
> 
> Dear Jeffrey,
> I know that we are not in contact, and whatever your
> reasons for not
> speaking to me may be I respect and understand your
> wish. I apologize if
> I have offended you in any way.
> I do not expect or need a response from you but I am
> simply writing  to
> wish you well. Because of what I have been reading
> in the press, I have
> been thinking of you lately, and out of friendship I
> am concerned and
> sincerely hope you are okay.
>  I want you to know that  I am, and always will be
> grateful for all that
> you have done for me during the years that we have
> known each other,
> from showing me around Harvard with Alan Dershowitz,
> to a weekend on the
> island, to the work opportunity with Charlie Rose.
> I will not forget the favors you have done for me.
> They have in small
> and big ways have had a positive impact on my life.
> And without the power or the resources to help you
> in any way (not that
> you need any help), I simply write to tell you that
> you have always been
> kind and decent towards me. I am certain that so
> many feel the same way.
> Regards,
> Bonnie
> Sent via BlackBerry from Cingular Wireless  
>

body_html:
thanks

--- Lesley Groff lesley@nysgllc.com wrote:

Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein

-----Original Message-----
From: Bonnie Hung [mailto:bhung@mba2007.hbs.edu]
Sent: Friday, August 11, 2006 12:50 PM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: For Jeffrey Epstein

Dear Jeffrey,
I know that we are not in contact, and whatever your
reasons for not
speaking to me may be I respect and understand your
wish. I apologize if
I have offended you in any way.
I do not expect or need a response from you but I am
simply writing to
wish you well. Because of what I have been reading
in the press, I have
been thinking of you lately, and out of friendship I
am concerned and
sincerely hope you are okay.
I want you to know that I am, and always will be
grateful for all that
you have done for me during the years that we have
known each other,
from showing me around Harvard with Alan Dershowitz,
to a weekend on the
island, to the work opportunity with Charlie Rose.
I will not forget the favors you have done for me.
They have in small
and big ways have had a positive impact on my life.
And without the power or the resources to help you
in any way (not that
you need any help), I simply write to tell you that
you have always been
kind and decent towards me. I am certain that so
many feel the same way.
Regards,
Bonnie
Sent via BlackBerry from Cingular Wireless
